question: When is the magnetic compass most effective?
options: In the region of the magnetic North Pole.
About midway between the magnetic poles.
On the geographic Equator.
In the region of the magnetic South Pole.
true: About midway between the magnetic poles.

question: With an increase in magnetic latitude there will be a decrease in the…
options: directive force.
vertical component of the Earth’s magnetic field.
total magnetic force of the Earth’s magnetic field.
angle of dip.
true: directive force.

question: The long-term periodic change in the Earth’s magnetic field…
options: mainly affects compass deviation.
is reflected in the slow movement of the magnetic poles.
is caused by sunspot activity.
is caused by the westerly movement of the geographic North Pole.
true: is reflected in the slow movement of the magnetic poles.

question: The horizontal component of the earth's magnetic field:
options: Weakens with increasing distance from the magnetic poles
Is approximately the same at all magnetic latitudes less than 60°
Is stronger closer to the magnetic equator
Weakens with increasing distance from the nearer magnetic pole
true: Is stronger closer to the magnetic equator

question: The Directive Force…
options: is about twice as strong on 60°N/S as on the Equator.
equals the vertical component of the Earth’s magnetic field.
is zero over the geographical poles.
is the component of the Earth’s magnetic field which aligns the compass needle.
true: is the component of the Earth’s magnetic field which aligns the compass needle.

question: Compass deviation is defined as the angle between…
options: Magnetic North and Compass North.
True North and Magnetic North.
True North and Compass North.
the horizontal and the total intensity of the Earth’s magnetic field.
true: Magnetic North and Compass North.

question: In a remote indicating compass system the amount of deviation caused by aircraft magnetism and electrical circuits may be minimised by:
options: Mounting the detector unit (flux valve) in the wingtip.
Using a vertically mounted gyroscope instead of a horizontally mounted one.
Mounting the flux valve in the cockpit.
Positioning the gyroscope in the centre of the aircraft.
true: Mounting the detector unit (flux valve) in the wingtip.

question: When an aircraft on a westerly heading in the Northern Hemisphere accelerates, the effect of the acceleration error causes the magnetic compass  to:
options: To turn faster than the actual turning rate of the aircraft
Indicate a turn towards the south
Lag behind the turning rate of the aircraft
Indicate a turn towards the north
true: Indicate a turn towards the north

question: An aircraft is over position HO (55°30’N 060°15’W), where YYR VOR (53°30’N 060°15’W) can be received. The magnetic variation is 31°W at HO and 28°W at YYR. What is the radial from YYR?
The annunciator of a remote indicating compass system is used when:
options: Synchronising the magnetic and gyro compass elements
Compensating for deviation
Setting local Magnetic Variation
Setting the 'heading' pointer
true: Synchronising the magnetic and gyro compass elements

question: An aircraft in the Northern Hemisphere is making an accurate rate one turn to the right. If the initial heading was 135°, after 30 seconds the direct reading magnetic compass should read:
options: Less than 225º
More or less than 225º depending on the pendulous suspension used
More than 225º
true: More than 225º

question:
Given: Variation is 6°W. Isogonic lines Jan
2002. Average annual increase 10'. Calculate: Variation in 2005.
Which of  the following statements about hard and soft iron in relation to magnetism is correct?
options: Both hard and soft hard iron are of a permanent nature.
Both hard and soft iron are of a non-permanent nature
Hard iron is of a non-permanent nature and soft iron is of a permanent nature
Hard iron magnetism is of a permanent nature and soft iron is of a non-permanent natüre
true: Hard iron magnetism is of a permanent nature and soft iron is of a non-permanent natüre

question: Magnetic compass calibration is carried out to reduce:
options: Parallax
Acceleration Errors
Variation
Deviation
true: Deviation

question: Permanent magnetism in aircraft arises chiefly from:
options: Exposure to the earth's magnetic field during normal operation
The effect of internal wiring and exposure to electrical storms
The combined effect of aircraft electrical equipment and the earth's magnetic field
Hammering, and the effect of the earth's magnetic field, whilst under construction
true: Hammering, and the effect of the earth's magnetic field, whilst under construction

question: The main reason for usually mounting the detector unit of a remote indicating compass in the wingtip of an aeroplane is to:
options: Place it in a position where there is no electrical wiring to cause deviation errors
Reduce the amount of deviation caused by aircraft magnetism and electrical circuits
Facilitate easy maintenance of the unit and increase its exposure to the Earth's magnetic field
Place it where it will not be subjected to electrical or magnetic interference from the aircraft
true: Reduce the amount of deviation caused by aircraft magnetism and electrical circuits

question: A direct reading magnetic compass can be checked for accuracy:
options: On a navigation route, to an accuracy of +/- 2°
in non-accelerating straight flight
At any point, by reference to the direction indicator
When lined up on the runway
true: When lined up on the runway

question: The sensitivity of a direct reading compass varies:
options: Inversely with the horizontal component of the earth's magnetic field
Directly with the horizontal component of the earth's magnetic field and inversely with the vertical component.
Directly with the vertical component of the earth's magnetic field
Inversely with both vertical and horizontal components of the earth's magnetic field
true: Directly with the horizontal component of the earth's magnetic field and inversely with the vertical component.

question: Which of the following statements concerning the earth's magnetic field is completely correct?
options: The earth's magnetic field can be classified as transient, semi-permanent or permanent
The blue pole of the earth's magnetic field is situated in North Canada
Dip is the angle between total magnetic field and vertical field component
At the earth's magnetic equator, the inclination varies depending on whether the geograhic equator is north or south of the magnetic equator
true: The blue pole of the earth's magnetic field is situated in North Canada

question: The purpose of compass check swing is to:
options: Cancel out the horizontal component of the earth's magnetic field
Measure the angle between Magnetic North and Compass North
Cancel out the effects of the magnetic fields found on board the aeroplane
Cancel out the vertical component of the earth's magnetic field
true: Measure the angle between Magnetic North and Compass North

question: At the magnetic equator, when accelerating after take off on heading West, a direct reading pivot suspended compass:
options: Underreads the heading
Indicates a turn to the south
Indicates the correct heading
Overreads the heading
true: Indicates the correct heading

question: Concerning direct reading magnetic compasses, in the Northern Hemisphere, it can be said that:
options: On an Easterly heading, a longitudinal acceleration causes an apparent turn to the South
On a Westerly heading, a longitudinal deceleration causes an apparent turn to the North
On an Easterly heading, a longitudinal acceleration causes an apparent turn to the North
On a Westerly heading, a longitudinal acceleration causes an apparent turn to the South
true: On an Easterly heading, a longitudinal acceleration causes an apparent turn to the North

question: In Northern Hemisphere, during an acceleration in an easterly direction, the magnetic compass will indicate:
options: An increase in heading
A heading of East
A decrease in heading
An apparent turn to the South
true: A decrease in heading

question: The main advantage of a remote indicating compass over a direct reading compass is that it:
options: Requires less maintenance
Senses, rather than seeks, the magnetic meridian
Is able to magnify the earth's magnetic field in order to attain greater accuracy
Has less moving parts
true: Senses, rather than seeks, the magnetic meridian

question: The main reason for mounting the detector unit of a remote reading compass in the wingtip of an aeroplane is:
options: To minimise the amount of deviation caused by aircraft magnetism and electrical circuits
To maximise the units exposure to the earth's magnetic field
By having detector units on both wingtips, to cancel out the deviation effects caused by the aircraft structure
To ensure that the unit is in the most accessible position on the aircraft for ease of maintenance
true: To minimise the amount of deviation caused by aircraft magnetism and electrical circuits

question: To ensure that the unit is in the most accessible position on the aircraft for ease of maintenance One purpose of a compass calibration is to reduce the difference, if any, between:
options: True North and Magnetic North.
Compass North and the lubber line.
Compass North and Magnetic North.
Compass North and True North.
true: Compass North and Magnetic North.

question: One purpose of compass calibration is to determine the deviation:
options: On any heading
At any latitude
On N, S, E and W only
On a given heading
true: On any heading

question: The force acting on the needle of a direct reading compass varies:
options: Directly with the horizontal component of the earth's magnetic field
Inversely with both vertical and horizontal components of the earth's magnetic field
Directly with the vertical component of the earth's magnetic field
Inversely with the horizontal component of the earth's magnetic field
true: Directly with the horizontal component of the earth's magnetic field

question: An aircraft is lined up on Runway 24, which is aligned with a magnetic bearing of 242°M.  In order to comply with maximum permissible deviation errors, the direct reading magnetic compass readings should be between:
An aircraft is purchased in New Zealand and taken to operate from Teeside.  The compass needs to be swung; this is because:
options: The aircraft had to travel through an area of intense Tropical Revolving Storms
The new owner fitted a new radio
The previous owner had not had it swung in the past 60 days
There was a large change of latitude
true: There was a large change of latitude

question: Given this deviation table of a Direct Reading Compass. To fly  000 030 060 090 120 150 180 210 240 270 300 330 Steer  359 030 061 092 121 150 178 209 242 272 298 331 The Direct Reading Compass indicates a heading 242°. The variation in the area is 22°E. What is the True Heading of the aircraft?

Direct reading compass (DRC) deviation table is: Course: 000 030 060 090 120 150 180 210 240 270 300 330 Steer:    359 030 061 092 121 150 178 209 242 272 298 331  You have to maintain the heading 155°(T). Magnetic variation is 10°W. The compass heading to follow is:

During ground checks the direct reading compass indication can best be compared with:

1. the runway direction upon aligning the aircraft.
2. the indication of the main compass system.
3. the indication of the local test VOR. 4.an ADF bearing.
5. a bearing on the airborne weather radar. Which of the preceding statements are true?
options: Only 2 and 3 are true.
All five statements are true.
Only 1 and 2 are true.
Only 4 and 5 are true.
true: Only 1 and 2 are true.

question: The horizontal component of the earth's magnetic field:
options: Is approximately the same at all magnetic latitudes less than 60°
Weakens with increasing distance from the nearer magnetic pole
Is approximately the same at magnetic latitudes 50°N and 50°S
Weakens with increasing distance from the magnetic poles
true: Is approximately the same at magnetic latitudes 50°N and 50°S

question: Which statement is true regarding a compass when directly overhead the north magnetic pole?
options: The horizontal component of the earths magnetic field is horizontal
The compass tail points down
The Magnetic Variation is 90º
The compass tip will point directly down
true: The compass tip will point directly down

question: An aircraft is lined up on Runway 05, which is aligned with a magnetic bearing of 047°M.  In order to comply with maximum permissible deviation errors, the direct reading magnetic compass readings should be between:

An aircraft is lined up on Runway 23, which is aligned with a magnetic bearing of 232°M.  During pre-flight checks it is observed that the direct reading magnetic compass reads 243°M.  What action should be taken by the flight crew in order to comply with maximum permissible deviation errors?
options: Adjust compass +° prior to continuing the flight
Continue the flight with no adjustments required
Cancel the flight and return to maintenance
Adjust compass -° prior to continuing the flight
true: Cancel the flight and return to maintenance

question: Which of the following methods is used pre-flight to check your direct reading compass?
options: Comparing the Test VOR reading with the compass heading
Comparing the compass heading with the known runway heading when lined up on the runway
Comparing the ADF bearing with the compass heading in flight
Comparing the ADF bearing with the compass heading on the runway
true: Comparing the compass heading with the known runway heading when lined up on the runway

question: Which of the following is an advantage of using a Direct- Reading Compass (DRC)?
options: The DRC gives an indication of True Heading
The DRC  needs no power supply
The DRC is the most accurate compass system available
The DRC is not affected by turns and accelerations
true: The DRC  needs no power supply
